data.title: Heritage Distilling registers 10M shares for resale under $15M equity facility
data.description:
• Heritage Distilling (NASDAQ:CASK) is registering up to 10M shares for resale by C/M Capital Master Fund.
  • The shares relate to a $15M equity line of credit under a January 2025 purchase agreement.
  • The company may sell shares to the investor at fluctuating market prices; it won’t receive resale proceeds.
  • Heritage may receive up to $15M in gross proceeds from primary sales to the investor.
  • Source: SEC filing

data.title: Bonterra upsizes private placement to $10.5M
data.description:
• Bonterra Resources (TSXV:BTR:CA) said on Friday that it will increase the size of its private placement.
  • The offering will now consist of of up to 22.72M “hard-dollar” units of the company at a price of $0.22 per HD unit, up to 14.58M “flow-through” units of the company at a price of C$0.24 per FT unit and up to 6.56M “flow-through” units of the company at a price of C$0.305 per premium FT unit, for aggregate for gross proceeds of up to around $10.5M.
  • The company intends to use the net proceeds from the offering to fund ongoing operations for the next twelve months, all as further detailed in the offering document.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Ventas announces pricing of $500M senior notes offering
data.description:
• Ventas' (NYSE:VTR) wholly owned subsidiary, Ventas Realty has priced an underwritten public offering of $500M aggregate principal amount of 5.1% senior notes due 2032 at an issue price equal to 99.391% of the principal amount of the notes.
  • The notes will be senior unsecured obligations of Ventas Realty and will be fully and unconditionally guaranteed by the Company and will mature on July 15, 2032.
  • The sale of the Notes is expected to close on June 3, 2025.
  • The company intends to use the proceeds from the offering of the notes for general corporate purposes.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: EchoStar rallies as Trump intervenes to resolve spat with FCC -- Bloomberg
data.description:
Shares of EchoStar (NASDAQ:SATS) were launched more than 50% higher in Friday’s late trading on reports that President Trump intervened to facilitate a resolution between the Federal Communications Commission and the company regarding its spectrum licenses.

According to sources cited by Bloomberg, EchoStar (SAT) chairman Charlie Ergen met with FCC chairman Brendan Carr on Wednesday, at which time Carr told Ergen to begin selling some of its spectrum licenses before losing them.

Last month, the FCC threatened to launch a probe into the company’s compliance with government requirements for 5G networks, raising questions as to EchoStar’s (NASDAQ:SATS) use of cellular and satellite spectrum licenses for airwaves.

EchoStar (NASDAQ:SATS) has been trying to build a nationwide communications network that would compete with AT&T (T), Verizon (VZ) and T-Mobile (TMUS), but has moved too slowly, leading the FCC to accuse the company of “spectrum warehousing.”

The FCC's probe follows SpaceX’s call for more satellite spectrum for Starlink. EchoStar, which has licenses to provide mobile device links, lacks the dense network of modern satellites that Starlink has at its disposal.

The FCC probe prompted EchoStar (SATS) to consider filing for Chapter 11 bankruptcy as protection from regulatory actions, claiming the FCC threats have “effectively frozen our ability to make decisions” about how to grow and invest in its Boost Mobile business.

Following Ergen’s meeting with Carr on Wednesday, Ergen met with Trump at the White House to discuss options. To avoid an EchoStar (SATS) bankruptcy, Trump called Carr to the Oval Office to hammer out a deal between the FCC and EchoStar (SATS).

data.title: REGENXBIO registers 268K shares for resale
data.description:
• REGENXBIO (NASDAQ:RGNX) is registering up to 268,096 shares of common stock for resale by a selling stockholder.
  • Shares are issuable upon exercise of a warrant priced at $14.92, issued in connection with a May 2025 loan agreement.
  • The company will not receive sale proceeds, but may receive up to ~$4M if the warrant is exercised in full for cash.
  • Source: SEC filing

data.title: 23andMe sold to non-profit headed by co-founder Anne Wojcicki
data.description:
• TTAM Research Institute, a nonprofit public benefit corporation run by 23andMe (OTC:MEHCQ) co-founder and former CEO Anne Wojcicki, will acquire nearly all the assets of that company for $305M.
  • TTAM was able to reach an agreement after outbidding Regeneron Pharmaceuticals (NASDAQ:REGN), which had previously agreed to buy 23andMe for $256M. Earlier this month, 23andMe asked for a second auction after Wojcicki offered $305M to buy her company back.
  • In addition, TTAM has committed to additional consumer protections and privacy safeguards to boost customer data and privacy.
  • "We believe it is critical that individuals are empowered to have choice and transparency with respect to their genetic data and have the opportunity to continue to learn about their ancestry and health risks as they wish," Wojcicki said in a statement.
  • A bankruptcy court hearing is scheduled for June 17 to discuss approving the deal, which if done, would close in the coming weeks.

data.title: Court extends pause in Nippon Steel case, with US Steel deal still pending
data.description:
The D.C. Circuit Court of Appeals on Friday extended a pause in litigation between Nippon Steel (OTCPK:NISTF) (OTCPK:NPSCY) and the Trump administration, and directed the two sides to provide the court with an update by June 20 about any future legal proceedings in the case over the company's US Steel (NYSE:X) takeover bid.

Such a filing could ask the court to continue to keep the case on pause, or ask the judges to set a new schedule.

Nippon Steel (OTCPK:NISTF) (OTCPK:NPSCY) and US Steel (NYSE:X) have been adding the finishing touches on an agreement with the Trump administration to assuage national security concerns posed by the merger that would allow the transaction to move forward.

US Steel (X) shares closed -2.8% on Friday after a Nippon Steel (OTCPK:NISTF) (OTCPK:NPSCY) executive told Japan's Nikkei newspaper that the planned merger would require "a degree of management freedom" to move forward.

The comments appeared directed at remarks made by President Trump on Thursday that the U.S. government would have control over US Steel (X) via a "golden share" that provides a 51% stake in the company.

data.title: KULR Technology to implement 1-for-8 reverse stock split
data.description:
• KULR Technology (NYSE:KULR) will enact a 1-for-8 reverse stock split effective June 23, 2025.
  • The move aims to attract institutional investors and align with the upcoming Russell 3000 reconstitution.
  • Post-split, shares outstanding will drop from ~300M to under 40M.
  • KULR shares fall 1.7% post-market on Friday.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: KalVista said FDA will not meet review date for sebetralstat
data.description:
KalVista Pharmaceuticals (NASDAQ:KALV) shares fell after the FDA has informed the company that it will not meet the review deadline for its oral on-demand treatment for hereditary angioedema, sebetralstat.

The agency informed the company on June 13, that it would not meet the previously announced PDUFA goal date of June 17, due to significant workload and resource constraints. The FDA expects to make a decision in about four weeks.

The company said that the FDA has not requested additional data or studies and has not raised any concerns regarding the safety, efficacy or approvability of sebetralstat.

The stock of the company is down about 3% post-market hours on Friday.

data.title: FCPT acquires Tires Plus property for $1.7M
data.description:
• Four Corners Property Trust (NYSE:FCPT) on Friday announced the acquisition of a Tires Plus property for $1.7M.
  • The transaction was priced at a 7.4% cap rate on rent as of the closing date and exclusive of transaction costs.
  • Source: Press Release
